tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly chronic kidney disease. Hyperkalemia and low CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, bilirubin, low specific gravity, and abnormal color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying etiology and management of renal failure and electrolyte disturbances.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
